data.title: Scorpio Tankers declares options for two more newbuildings, lifting Dalian order to four ships
data.description:
Scorpio Tankers (STNG) said Thursday it declared options to purchase two scrubber-fitted LR2 newbuilding product tankers to be constructed at China's Dalian Shipbuilding for $68.5M per vessel, with deliveries expected in Q3 and Q4 2029.

The decision doubles Scorpio's (STNG) order at the yard to four vessels and raises the company's overall newbuilding orderbook to 10 ships.

The latest order builds on a fleet renewal move unveiled in December, when Scorpio (STNG) agreed to sell two LR2 product tankers for $52.3M each while also lining up two scrubber-fitted LR2 newbuildings at Dalian for $70.8M per vessel, with delivery set for Q3 2027.

Scorpio (STNG) currently operates a fleet of 91 product tankers; beyond the four LR2s at Dalian, its orderbook includes four MR tankers due for delivery in 2026 and 2027, as well as two VLCCs slated for H2 2028.

The company revealed the move alongside Q4 adjusted earnings that beat analyst estimates and a 7% increase in quarterly dividends to $0.45/share.

data.title: Sphere Entertainment outlines 4-year timeline for National Harbor venue and targets global expansion backed by $200M incentives
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sphere Entertainment Co. (SPHR) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Executive Chairman & CEO James Dolan highlighted progress on Sphere’s business model, emphasizing momentum from the Las Vegas venue and the Wizard of Oz production as “an important blueprint for our long-term vision, a global network of spear venues powered by our proprietary technology and immersive content.” Dolan announced a second U.S. Sphere venue at National Harbor in Maryland, targeting opening within four years and supported by $200 million in state, local, and private incentives. He noted, “We are moving quickly to finalize agreements and secure necessary approvals and believe the venue could be open in 4 years or less.”
  • Dolan reported that preconstruction in Abu Dhabi is in its final stages, with updates on site location expected soon, and stated the company is “in active discussions with a significant number of domestic and international markets regarding large and smaller scale Spheres.”
  • The Wizard of Oz show continues to perform strongly, with over 2.2 million tickets sold and approximately $290 million in ticket sales. Dolan announced plans to release “Wizard of Oz 2.0, an enhanced version of the production with new scenes and new 4D effect.”
  • Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Robert Langer stated, “For the December quarter, we generated total company revenues of $394.3 million and adjusted operating income of $128 million.” Langer detailed that the Sphere segment’s revenues rose to $274.2 million, driven by the Wizard of Oz and increased performances, along with growth in concert residencies and Exosphere advertising and sponsorship.
  • Langer also noted, “Fourth quarter adjusted operating income for our Sphere segment was $89.4 million as compared to an adjusted operating loss of approximately $800,000 in the prior year quarter,” citing increased revenues and lower SG&A expenses, partially offset by higher direct operating expenses.

Outlook

  • Dolan indicated ongoing expansion efforts, mentioning, “over the next few years, I don’t think you should be surprised by the 5 or 6 projects going on at once,” with a focus on separating project financing and utilizing various funding sources.
  • The company expects the National Harbor venue to open in four years or less, pending approvals. Dolan said, “We will update you on our progress over the course of the year.”
  • Koester, President & COO, expressed confidence in Las Vegas performance despite seasonal headwinds and cited a strategy of “aggressively putting forward days where we have multiple shows...to enhance and grow revenue per day.”
  • Langer commented on cost management, stating, “Looking to '26 and beyond, we will absolutely continue to look for further cost saving opportunities wherever it makes sense.”

Financial Results

  • The company reported total revenues of $394.3 million for the quarter, with the Sphere segment contributing $274.2 million. Adjusted operating income for the Sphere segment was $89.4 million, up from an adjusted operating loss in the prior year.
  • MSG Networks generated $120.1 million in revenues and $38.6 million in AOI, with Langer noting a 14.5% decrease in subscribers and lower affiliate rates impacting results.
  • SG&A expenses for the quarter were $104.1 million, a decrease of $14.9 million year-over-year, including $4.6 million in executive management transition costs.
  • Sphere business net debt stood at $56 million as of December 31, with $477 million in unrestricted cash and cash equivalents.
  • The company refinanced its Las Vegas credit facility, extending maturity to 2031 and adding a $275 million undrawn revolver.

Q&A

  • Brandon Ross, LightShed Partners, LLC, asked about the capacity for expansion projects. Dolan replied, “...over the next few years, I don't think you should be surprised by the 5 or 6 projects going on at once. Hopefully in the best markets. But if we can figure out how to do more and there's more opportunity, we're going to try and take advantage of it.”
  • Ross inquired about construction costs. Dolan responded, “...the model still very much holds up to support that level of investment. I'm hoping we bring it in for less...there's also new construction methods, which help lower costs.”
  • David Karnovsky, JPMorgan, asked for details on the National Harbor location and financing. Dolan explained, “...we got a very good offer kind of really great location, and we took it...there's a lot of different ways that we can do it...our lending institutions are bullish on our projects.”
  • Stephen Laszczyk, Goldman Sachs, asked about ticket sales amid seasonal trends. Koester answered, “We've seen Las Vegas headwinds our way, but we've been resilient and experienced strong growth despite headwinds this past year, and we feel confident that we'll continue to do the same in the next year.”
  • Laszczyk also questioned SG&A outlook. Langer stated, “...we are extremely focused on managing our cost infrastructure as efficiently as possible... adjusting for these items, our SG&A expenses in the quarter are actually quite similar to levels we saw for the rest of 2025.”
  • Joseph Stauff, Susquehanna, asked about the timeline for "From the Edge". Dolan said, “I suspect that, that will debut from The Edge sometime in the fourth quarter, that they could slip into the first quarter.”
  • Logan Angress, Wolfe Research, asked about cannibalization between venues. Dolan replied, “I really don't see that...these markets rates can certainly handle that...I don't really see one market disturbing the other.”
  • Ryan Sigdahl, Craig-Hallum, inquired about IP pipeline. Dolan stated, “...every IP holder that we talk to is incredibly enthusiastic about taking their IP and putting it into this new medium.”
  • Peter Henderson, BofA Securities, asked about the residency pipeline. Dolan noted, “we're pretty much booked...I don't think there's hardly any left in '26.”
  • David Joyce, Seaport Research, asked about sponsorship strategy. Koester responded, “...our performance at CES this year, our year-over-year growth was strong...we debuted the first interactive game experience on the Exosphere in partnership with LEGO and Lucas' film, Star Wars.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was generally positive, focusing on expansion plans, content pipeline, and cost management, with questions aimed at clarifying project timelines, financing strategies, and demand sustainability. There was some probing about construction costs and capacity, but the sentiment remained constructive.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently referencing strong market demand and operational resilience. Phrases such as “we feel confident that we'll continue to do the same in the next year” and “we are extremely focused on managing our cost infrastructure as efficiently as possible” signaled assurance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence increased, with more explicit forward-looking comments on expansion and product pipeline. Analysts’ sentiment remained constructive, with a greater emphasis on project execution and scalability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw the announcement of a new U.S. Sphere venue, a more specific timeline for expansion, and a significant increase in Sphere segment revenue and operating income compared to the previous period.
  • Management shifted from emphasizing technology and early content success in Q3 to a broader focus on geographic expansion, content diversification, and capital allocation in Q4.
  • Analysts’ questions evolved from technology monetization and content performance to expansion capacity, construction costs, and financing structure, reflecting growing interest in scalability and project execution.
  • Management’s tone was notably more optimistic, highlighting momentum and concrete plans for multiple venues, as opposed to the more cautious exploration of opportunities in the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management mentioned construction costs as a potential challenge, but Dolan stated, “the model still very much holds up to support that level of investment. I'm hoping we bring it in for less.”
  • SG&A fluctuations and executive transition costs were noted, but Langer emphasized ongoing cost efficiency efforts.
  • Subscriber declines and affiliate rate reductions impacted MSG Networks, with Langer acknowledging a 14.5% decrease in subscribers.
  • Analysts raised questions on financing gaps for new projects and the potential for venue cannibalization, but management expressed confidence in market capacity and financing options.

Final Takeaway

Sphere Entertainment Co. closed fiscal 2025 with robust revenue growth driven by the success of the Wizard of Oz show, while laying out an ambitious expansion strategy with the announcement of a new $1 billion venue at National Harbor and ongoing developments in Abu Dhabi. Management expressed confidence in the company’s ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously, supported by strong demand, diversified content, and a focus on cost efficiency. The company’s operational momentum and progress towards building a global network of Sphere venues signal substantial long-term growth opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: ClearBridge Mid Cap Strategy adds CWST and TYL; exits CPAY and DKNG
data.description:
• ClearBridge Mid Cap Strategy underperformed its Russell Midcap Index during the fourth quarter.
  • In Q4 2025, initiated new positions in Casella Waste Systems (NASDAQ:CWST) and Tyler Technologies (NYSE:TYL).
  • During  Q4, the fund exited Corpay (NYSE:CPAY) and DraftKings (NASDAQ:DKNG).
  • Source.

data.title: U.S. importers bear the brunt of tariffs, NY Fed says
data.description:
Despite President Donald Trump's insistence that other countries are paying the tariffs that his administration imposed on goods coming into the U.S., research from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York released on Thursday found that most tariffs are being borne by the companies importing the goods.

During the course of 2025, though, some of the cost of the duties did shift to exporters, they suggest. The year started with an average tariff rate of 2.6% spiked in April and May and ended the year at 13%, the New York Fed researchers said.

For the first eight months of last year, 94% of the tariff cost fell to U.S. importers, with the rest on foreign exporters. In November, though, the proportion shouldered by U.S. importers declined to 86%, meaning 14% was carried by the exporters.

"In sum, U.S. firms and consumers continue to bear the bulk of the economic burden of the high tariffs imposed in 2025," the researchers said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Mortgage rates inch down, housing affordability continues to improve
data.description:
This week, mortgage rates inched down and housing affordability continued to improve, showed the latest Freddie Mac (FMCC) Primary Mortgage Survey.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.09% as of February 12, down from 6.11% last week and 6.87% in the same period a year ago.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.44%, compared to 5.50% a week ago and 6.09% in the prior year.

"Bolstered by strong economic growth, a solid labor market and mortgage rates at three-year lows, housing affordability continues to measurably improve," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.

"These factors have caught the attention of many prospective homebuyers, driving purchase application activity higher than a year ago," added Khater.

Here is a look at this week's survey results:

data.title: Waymo to launch AVs with its most advanced, 6th-generation driver
data.description:
Alphabet’s Waymo (GOOG) (GOOGL) is raising the bar on autonomous driving with the launch of its most advanced driver technology, built to handle even more treacherous road conditions and accelerate the large-scale deployment of its AVs.

The 6th generation Waymo Driver was developed using data from nearly 200 million fully autonomous miles across the dense urban cores of more than 10 major cities and expanding network of freeways.

“By leveraging breakthroughs in AI and validating the system through our rigorous safety framework, we can now accelerate our journey to the road with unprecedented velocity and confidence. Today, we're lifting the lid on our 6th-generation Waymo Driver's sophisticated sensing technology delivering expanded capabilities at a lower cost,” the company said in a statement on its website.

Through advancements in Waymo’s vision systems (lidar and radar) and external audio receivers ((EARs)), the 6th generation Waymo Driver now “operates at a level superior to human eyes and ears” that can pull critical details out of deep shadows while being hit with bright lights from high beams or emergency vehicles.

Through the use of Waymo’s next-generation 17-megapixel imager, the driver can capture millions of data points with fewer cameras and remain obstruction-free in snow and rain conditions.

Additionally, in conditions where a camera’s view may be limited, Waymo’s lidar and radar provide the necessary redundancy to maintain the driver’s perception.

To complement more sophisticated cameras, Waymo’s EARs can detect important sounds on the road while at the same time reduce the amount of wind noise, making it more effective in determining where the sound is coming from before it obtains a visual location. 

The 6th generation system also marks a major shift at the company’s AV factory in Metro, Phoenix, to allow for a “meaningful” scale towards its goal of tens of thousands of units per year.  

News of Waymo’s most advanced AV is weighing on Tesla (TSLA), Baidu (BIDU), Pony.ai (PONY), and Mobileye (MBLY), amplifying broader pressure on the technology sector.

data.title: Crane NXT outlines 4%–6% 2026 sales growth as international currency demand accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Crane NXT (CXT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Aaron Saak, President, CEO & Director, opened by highlighting a strong finish to the year with “sales growth was approximately 20% in the fourth quarter and 11% for the full year,” and “adjusted EBITDA margin was approximately 25% in Q4 and 24% for the full year.” Saak emphasized the company’s achievement of “20 new currency denomination wins specifying our micro-optics technology” in 2025, exceeding its target of 10 to 15, and noted these wins included “5 new denominations for the nation of Fiji.” Saak stated, “with our currency team's continued streak of wins and organic backlog up more than 30% year-over-year, we are highly confident in our sales outlook for this business in 2026.”
  • Saak also highlighted the completed equipment upgrades for the new U.S. currency series, with the new $10 bill set for release later in 2026, and celebrated major contract renewals in the Crane Authentication business, including new multi-year agreements with Major League Baseball and the NFL.
  • The creation of Crane Authentication through the integration of optic security and De La Rue Authentication was described as a step to drive margin accretion in 2026, with continued progress on synergy realization.
  • The company closed its initial equity investment in Antares Vision and plans to take the company private by mid-2026.
  • Christina Cristiano, Senior VP & CFO, reported, “sales were $477 million in the quarter, an increase of approximately 20% year-over-year,” with core sales growth driven by “accelerating growth in SAT, partially offset by expected softness in CPI.” Cristiano noted, “adjusted segment operating margin of approximately 26% declined approximately 120 basis points versus the prior year,” and “adjusted free cash flow conversion was very strong at approximately 135%.”

Outlook

  • For 2026, Crane NXT expects “full year sales growth of 4% to 6%.” In SAT, management projects “high single-digit growth driven by high single-digit growth in U.S. currency” and “low single-digit growth in international currency, even with a tough comparison to a very strong 2025.” Crane Authentication is anticipated to see “mid-single-digit core growth, including a full year contribution from the De La Rue Authentication acquisition.”
  • CPI is expected to have “flat revenue year-over-year,” with “mid-single-digit growth in service” offset by “approximately flat revenue year-over-year in our hardware businesses and a low single-digit decline in vending.”
  • Cristiano stated, “we expect adjusted segment EBITDA margin to be approximately 28%, which is approximately flat year-over-year,” and “full year adjusted EPS in the range of $4.10 to $4.40.”
  • CapEx for 2026 is projected to increase to “approximately 7% of currency's revenue,” with overall CapEx spending for Crane NXT expected to remain “in the range of 3% to 5% of sales in total.”

Financial Results

  • The company reported “sales were $477 million in the quarter,” with “adjusted segment operating margin of approximately 26%.” Adjusted free cash flow conversion reached “approximately 135%,” and adjusted EPS for the quarter was “$1.27.”
  • Full-year sales were reported as “approximately $1.7 billion,” with a “core sales growth of approximately 1%” and “adjusted EPS of $4.06.”
  • The SAT segment delivered “core sales growth of approximately 7%,” driven by strength in currency, and the CPI segment maintained “adjusted operating margin at approximately 29%.”
  • Year-end net leverage was “approximately 2.3x,” and the company secured a term loan of “roughly $500 million” for the Antares Vision transaction.

Q&A

  • Matt Summerville, D.A. Davidson: Asked about SAT margin performance in Q4 and pricing elasticity. Saak responded that most contracts are booked in backlog well in advance and, “as new contracts come into the backlog, we are very focused on ensuring we are maximizing our value and the pricing power we have with our leading technology.”
  • Summerville: Inquired about EPS cadence and cost recovery. Cristiano explained, “EPS will accelerate through the first half. And then level off a little bit in the back half of the year to get to that total of $4.10 to $4.40.” Saak added that “we are going to continue to be on this march now of increasing EBITDA margin and significant expansion in SAT on a go-forward basis, highly confident of that.”
  • Michael Halloran, Baird: Questioned sequential CPI dynamics and recovery. Cristiano stated, “CPI is expected to be flat in 2026 with a 30% EBITDA margin and continued very strong free cash flow conversion.”
  • Halloran: Asked about $10 bill onboarding and international business flow. Saak stated, “we're just being prudent here on when they actually make the announcement,” with full consumer release likely in Q4 2026. Cristiano noted a “very tough comp in Q4 of 2026 based on the acceleration that we saw this year in Q4,” with profitability expected to improve as the year progresses.
  • Bob Labick, CJS: Inquired about international currency demand drivers and sustainability. Saak cited “increased counterfeiting,” “growth in emerging economies,” and “faster redesign times” as durable trends and expects to “continue to be in that range of 10 to 15 wins” annually.
  • Ian Zaffino, Oppenheimer: Asked for a breakout of SAT segment growth. Saak reported “currency was up high double digits in the fourth quarter,” mainly from international currency, and confirmed synergy activities ahead of schedule.
  • Robert Brooks, Northland: Asked about the financial benefit of professional sports league contracts. Cristiano described these as “a great recurring revenue stream” and “very sticky arrangements.” Brooks also asked about 24/7 staffing transitions; Saak explained ramp-up to “24/7, which will be at here in Q1.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral to slightly positive tone, seeking clarity on margin recovery, sustainability of growth, and operational execution. Their questions pressed for details on backlog, cadence, and capacity constraints but did not express significant skepticism.
  • Management’s tone was confident and optimistic, particularly regarding demand trends, backlog, and execution of growth initiatives. Saak frequently used phrases such as “highly confident” and “very positive about the long-term outlook,” and Cristiano described guidance as “balanced” and “prudent.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment remained steady, with slight increases in optimism due to strong reported results. Management’s confidence increased, emphasizing acceleration in growth and successful integration of acquisitions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is more detailed, with explicit projections for SAT, CPI, and Authentication, compared to a more preliminary outlook in Q3. The change from adjusted operating profit to adjusted EBITDA as the primary profitability metric was announced this quarter.
  • Management’s confidence in international currency and Authentication growth has strengthened, citing a more than 30% increase in organic backlog and synergy realization.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from questions about capacity investments and backlog management in Q3 to detailed inquiries on margin improvement and sustainability of growth in Q4.
  • Key metrics such as Q4 sales, adjusted EPS, and free cash flow conversion all increased versus Q3, while margin pressures from costs and FX were explicitly addressed in both quarters.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited continued costs and investments required to meet increased international currency demand, including staffing, higher freight, procurement of substrates, and outsourcing of printing. Unfavorable FX impacts were noted as a margin headwind.
  • CPI division faces ongoing softness in vending due to tariffs and delayed customer orders, and the guidance reflects caution in hardware revenue outlook.
  • The timing of the U.S. Treasury’s $10 bill release and the integration of the Antares Vision acquisition present uncertainty for 2026 guidance updates.

Final Takeaway

Crane NXT management highlighted a strong finish to 2025, driven by record wins in international currency, successful acquisition integration, and robust free cash flow. The company projects continued sales growth of 4% to 6% in 2026, supported by high confidence in backlog and demand for security technologies. Investments in capacity and technology are expected to drive long-term value, while management remains focused on margin improvement, prudent guidance, and disciplined capital allocation as it navigates ongoing industry challenges and prepares for further strategic expansion.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Solaris Energy most shorted energy stock in January, Cheniere Energy sees least interest
data.description:
In January, the S&P 500 energy sector (XLE) moved 11.8% in the upward direction, dwarfing the 1.2% rise in S&P500 (SP500).

In the last month, crude oil futures (CL1:COM) surged 13.8%, as the buildup of U.S. military forces in the Middle East heightened concerns that strikes against Iran could occur soon, potentially disrupting global crude supply.

Within the energy sector, Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) was the most shorted stock in January, while Cheniere Energy Partners (CQP) was the least shorted stock.

Most shorted energy stocks as of January-end:

  • Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) 28.24%
  • Centrus Energy (LEU) 23.35%
  • Delek US Holdings (DK) 20.67%
  • Northern Oil and Gas (NOG) 20.06%
  • PBF Energy (PBF) 17.16%
 

Least shorted energy stocks as of January-end:

  • Cheniere Energy Partners (CQP) 0.16%, unchanged from last month.
  • MPLX (MPLX) 0.72%
  • Alliance Resource Partners (ARLP) 0.89%
  • Mach Natural Resources (MNR) 0.94%
  • Energy Transfer (ET) 0.96%

data.title: Check Point falls after mixed Q4, announces 3 acquisitions done in Q1
data.description:
Shares of Check Point Software Technologies (CHKP) fell about 8% on Thursday after mixed fourth-quarter results.

The cybersecurity company also announced three acquisitions carried out in the first quarter of 2026 of Cyata, Cyclops, and Rotate.

Q4 Metrics

Revenue for the fourth quarter, ended Dec. 31, rose about 6% year-over-year to $745M, missing estimates. Meanwhile, non-GAAP EPS surged 26% year-over-year to $3.40, beating analysts' expectations.

Remaining Performance Obligation, or RPO, was about $2.73B, an 8% increase year over year.

"We delivered solid fourth quarter and full year 2025 results, with revenue landing above the midpoint of our outlook and EPS exceeding expectations. Our performance remained resilient throughout the year, driven by continued customer adoption across our Hybrid Mesh Network and Workspace platforms," said CEO Nadav Zafrir.

Revenue from Products and Licenses rose 0.7% year-over-year to $171.8M, while Security Subscriptions revenues climbed 11% year-on-year to $325.1M.

Calculated Billings reached about $1.04B, an 8% increase year over year.

Outlook

"In 2026, our strategy is centered on securing our customers’ AI transformation across the enterprise. We are focused on executing against our four strategic pillars, Hybrid Mesh, Workspace, and Exposure Management, while embedding AI driven security throughout our portfolio. Today’s announced acquisition of Cyata further expands our AI security stack, enabling full discovery, governance, and control of AI agents as organizations accelerate their AI journeys," said Zafrir.

The company expects first quarter 2026 EPS between $2.35 and $2.45 (midpoint at $2.40), compared to a consensus EPS estimate of $2.37.

Check Point forecasts revenue between $655M and $685M (midpoint at $670M) versus a consensus revenue estimate of $677.45M.

The company expects full year 2026 EPS between $10.05 and $10.85 (midpoint at $10.45), compared to a consensus EPS estimate of $10.48.

Check Point expects full year revenue between $2.85 and $2.95B (midpoint at $2.9B) versus a consensus revenue estimate of $2.90B.

Analysts Commentary

RBC Capital Markets kept its Sector Perform rating on Check Point's stock with a $195 price target.

"Check Point delivered good Q4/25 results to end the year, with billings ahead of consensus and revenue/EPS exceeding the midpoint of guidance. We note that revenue y/y growth decelerated by ~100 bps q/q. Operating margin came in lighter than expectations by around ~80 bps. Performance in the quarter was driven by continued customer adoption across the portfolio, including the Hybrid Mesh Network and Workspace platforms," said analysts led by Matthew Hedberg.

Acquisitions

Check Point said it acquired Cyata to help accelerate its mission to deliver an end-to-end AI security platform for the agentic world.

The company acquired Cyclops to expand its Exposure Management offering to deliver a complete Continuous Threat Exposure Management, or CTEM, solution to the market.

Check Point added that it acquired Rotate — an all-in-one platform purpose-built for managed service providers, or MSPs. The company noted that it acquired the talent of Rotate to accelerate the strong Workspace momentum in the MSP market.

Update: The story was updated with analysts' comments and stock price movement.

data.title: SoftBank-backed PayPay files for U.S. IPO after government shutdown delay
data.description:
PayPay Corp., a Japanese digital payment platform backed by SoftBank (SFTBY) (SFTBF), filed its preliminary prospectus for a U.S. initial public offering on Thursday. It expects to trade on the Nasdaq under the symbol "PAYP".

The IPO had originally been expected in December 2025 but was delayed by the U.S. government shutdown that spanned October into the first half of November, according to media reports.

The number of American Depositary Shares being offered and the expected price range have yet to be determined. SoftBank (SFTBY) is still expected to hold a controlling stake in PayPay after the offering is completed.

For the fiscal year ended March 31, 2025, PayPay's operating profit jumped to ¥35.5B (US$230M), up from ¥11M in FY2024 and -¥20.5B in FY2023. Its total gross merchandise volume grew 18% Y/Y to ¥15.7T (US$100B) in 2025. PayPay's number of transactions rose 23% Y/Y to 7,806.6M last year.

For the first nine months of fiscal 2026, its operating profit more than doubled to ¥61.0B. Total GMV for the same period increased 24% to ¥14.29T, according to the filing.
